text:
Inserting the low-angle rig shot file taken with 28mm as an insert over the Changdeokgung 28mm footage, and finally compositing the virtual character on top.

I pulled the color-graded version from the LOG file's color space. Up to today it's still doable work so I can just queue up batch processing, but the action scene I scheduled for the day before next week's business trip is stressing me out. Will it even recognize everything properly...

I still have to crunch through the camera tracking and that rolling severed head footage I shot earlier too...

I already put in all the visual scenes from the severed head's POV as it rolls on the ground, but (I literally rolled the camera. I made a wire structure based on one that wouldn't crush an egg, built it myself, mounted the camera inside, and rolled it across the ground.)

If I have to insert every single angle and size visible during that exact 0.9-second movement as images and paint out the reflections of the structure...

I feel like I'm gonna lose my mind before I even finish. lol
Just looking at the footage for a second now is making me dizzy...

It's the stuff I rolled while dying of heat at Gongju Sanseong.

Sigh... Will my local AI even survive this...?
Should I try to get it done before the business trip...? lololol

Think about it. Maybe when I get there I'll see?? If there's a GPU sale going on in the US, wouldn't it be smarter to just buy one and bring it back...? lol

What the hell am I saying. I've already bought the damn thing. Fuck.

Gue nemu website yang bikin gue mikir, "Ini harusnya gak gratis."

Namanya MeiGen.

Basically, ini adalah library prompt buat AI image dan video generation. Tapi bukan jenis prompt receh yang udah sering lo lihat sliweran di Twitter.

Semua di sini sifatnya curated, udah di-test, dan hasil output-nya udah masuk level produksi.

Lo bisa dapet prompt matang buat berbagai model top:
→ GPT Image 2
→ Seedance 2.0
→ Veo 3.1
→ Midjourney
→ Dan beberapa model besar lainnya

Yang bikin gue appreciate banget: prompt-nya bukan cuma sekelas "make a cool image." Banyak yang udah di-spec khusus buat kebutuhan cinematic lighting, product shot, fashion editorial, sampai anime style—hal-hal detail yang biasanya butuh trial & error berjam-jam buat dapetin formula pasnya.

Dan ini poin penting yang sering banget dilewatin orang:
Model AI-nya sendiri sekarang udah pada gila dan makin pinter. Yang sekarang jadi bottleneck itu bukan lagi tool-nya, tapi masalahnya lo gak tahu cara "ngomong" yang bener ke model tersebut.

Prompt engineering itu real skill. Dan website kayak MeiGen ini basically nge-shortcut learning curve lo buat dapet aset visual kualitas mumpuni.

To people in the answers saying "but opus 4.8 is weaker so without fallback, the score would even be higher": this is not necessarily true because of how any benchmark - which is an average of queries - work and what is called "the fallacy of division".

Even if Opus 4.8 has a lower average score on AA than Fable 5, it actually performs better than Fable 5 on some benchmark that compose the index of AA, especially where there's high refusal rate of Fable 5 (ex GPQA Diamond, AA-Omniscience). The same would go if you'd take a single benchmark btw as it's always an average of queries and the fact that a model has a higher score on average doesn't mean they answer better on 100% of queries.

So it's possible that Fable with Opus 4.8 fallbacks is getting a higher score than pure Fable, even if Opus 4.8 is weaker on average.

The challenge is no one knows, except the API provider, which is the challenge I'm pointing out.

More details below from Fable (or Opus?) themselves!

Can one diffusion model master multiple text-to-image tasks without forgetting?

Researchers from Fudan University & Alibaba Group present DiffusionOPD. 

Instead of joint training, they first train separate expert teachers, then distill their knowledge into a single student along its own rollout. This decouples learning and avoids cross-task interference.

Result: Outperforms both multi-reward RL and cascade RL baselines, achieving state-of-the-art on all benchmarks.

DiffusionOPD: A Unified Perspective of On-Policy Distillation in Diffusion Models 

Paper: https://t.co/j0ehhYqLOC
Project: https://t.co/JSUN5xMRUR
Code: https://t.co/yOJC1Ndm32

Our report: https://t.co/WjqtQhGfD0

📬 #PapersAccepted by Jiqizhixin

Diffusion (or flow) makes for excellent policies, but training them with RL is notoriously hard: BPTT is unstable, RL over diffusion blows up the horizon. In our new paper, we show how we can optimize flow matching actors by using "one weird trick" -- "approximate" the Jacobian of the flow denoising process with the identity matrix. 👇

This graph captures what’s broken about AI evals: they structurally favor closed-source APIs that can route, fallback, ensemble, and optimize behind the scenes with no transparency.

Most gen AI fashion ads look good, but they don’t move. The edit has no rhythm. Sound design straight from seedance generation.

So I made a Supreme x Evangelion spec ad to test the part everyone skips: fast cuts, beat matching, and satisfying sound design. https://t.co/VPB9WkdcY9
